overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 11.528s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 3 (+326.316s), where 7.3 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 3.02s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 63.0 km/h below reference (31.4 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Carry more speed to the apex — target 94 km/h. Take a wider entry to enable a later apex and trust the exit. This corner is where straight-line speed is won or lost.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 31.4 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-63.0 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 30.5 km/h below reference (35.0 vs 65.5 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 66 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 35.0 km/h vs 65.5 km/h reference (-30.5 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 7.3 km/h below reference (47.1 vs 54.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 54 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 47.1 km/h vs 54.4 km/h reference (-7.3 km/h).
